sourceText: Enduring subjugation under the Rouran Khaganate and suffering the ultimate humiliation when its ruler refused my marriage proposal, calling me his 'blacksmith slave'.
translatedText: Kuvumilia kutiishwa chini ya Rouran Khaganate na kuteseka kwa fedheha kuu wakati mtawala wake alipokataa ombi langu la ndoa, akiniita 'mtumwa wake mhunzi'.

sourceText: By uniting the Turkic tribes, forging a strategic alliance with the Western Wei dynasty, and decisively defeating the Rouran, I established my own Khaganate and claimed my rightful place.
translatedText: Kwa kuunganisha makabila ya Waturuki, kuunda muungano wa kimkakati na nasaba ya Wei Magharibi, na kuwashinda Warouran, nilianzisha Khaganate yangu na kudai mahali pangu panapostahili.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 6

sourceText: A brutal power struggle against my half-brother Yaropolk and the immense challenge of uniting disparate, pagan tribes.
translatedText: Mapambano makali ya nguvu dhidi ya kaka yangu wa kambo Yaropolk na changamoto kubwa ya kuunganisha makabila yaliyotofautiana, ya kipagani.

sourceText: Through military campaigns, strategic alliances, and ultimately the adoption of a single state religion, I forged a unified Kievan Rus' with a shared identity.
translatedText: Kupitia kampeni za kijeshi, ushirikiano wa kimkakati, na hatimaye kupitishwa kwa dini ya serikali moja, nilitengeneza Kievan Rus' yenye utambulisho wa pamoja.

sourceText: Late 10th - Early 11th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 10 - Mapema karne ya 11

sourceText: Vladimir Vernadsky
translatedText: Vladimir Vernadsky

sourceText: He had to continue his research amidst the chaos of the Russian Revolution and Civil War, facing political pressures that threatened his scientific freedom.
translatedText: Ilimbidi aendelee na utafiti wake katikati ya machafuko ya Mapinduzi ya Urusi na Vita vya wenyewe kwa wenyewe, akikabiliwa na shinikizo za kisiasa ambazo zilitishia uhuru wake wa kisayansi.

sourceText: Through his unwavering commitment to science and the support of his colleagues, he founded the Ukrainian Academy of Sciences and continued his research, developing his theories on the biosphere despite the turbulent times.
translatedText: Kupitia dhamira yake isiyoyumba kwa sayansi na usaidizi wa wenzake, alianzisha Chuo cha Sayansi cha Kiukreni na kuendelea na utafiti wake, akiendeleza nadharia zake juu ya biosphere licha ya nyakati za misukosuko.

sourceText: 19th-20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19-20

sourceText: Sergei Korolev
translatedText: Sergei Korolev

sourceText: Accused as an 'enemy of the people' during Stalin's Great Purge, I endured years of brutal imprisonment in a Gulag.
translatedText: Nilishtakiwa kuwa 'adui wa watu' wakati wa Mapambano Makuu ya Stalin, nilivumilia miaka mingi ya kufungwa kikatili katika Gulag.

sourceText: After my release, I became the Chief Designer of the Soviet space program, channeling my experiences into an unyielding drive for success.
translatedText: Baada ya kuachiliwa, nikawa Mbuni Mkuu wa programu ya anga za juu ya Usovieti, nikielekeza uzoefu wangu katika msukumo usiobadilika wa mafanikio.

sourceText: 20th Century Space Race
translatedText: Mbio za Nafasi za Karne ya 20

sourceText: Semyon Dezhnev
translatedText: Semyon Dezhnev

sourceText: The brutal Siberian wilderness, constant danger, loss of men and ships, and the fact his monumental discovery went unrecognized for nearly a century.
translatedText: Jangwa la kikatili la Siberia, hatari ya mara kwa mara, kupoteza watu na meli, na ukweli kwamba ugunduzi wake mkubwa haukutambuliwa kwa karibu karne.

sourceText: Through sheer resilience, survival skills, and a steadfast commitment to his duties, he endured. His legacy was secured when his detailed reports were finally rediscovered in the archives.
translatedText: Kupitia uthabiti kamili, ujuzi wa kuishi, na kujitolea kwa uthabiti kwa majukumu yake, alivumilia. Urithi wake ulilindwa wakati ripoti zake za kina hatimaye zilipatikana tena kwenye kumbukumbu.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Shota Rustaveli
translatedText: Shota Rustaveli

sourceText: The scarcity of historical records about my life, leaving my personal story shrouded in mystery.
translatedText: Uhaba wa rekodi za kihistoria kuhusu maisha yangu, ukiacha hadithi yangu ya kibinafsi iliyofunikwa na siri.

sourceText: Achieving immortality through my work, as my epic poem has preserved my philosophy and spirit for centuries.
translatedText: Kufikia kutokufa kupitia kazi yangu, kama shairi langu la epic limehifadhi falsafa na roho yangu kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 12th Century Georgian Golden Age
translatedText: Umri wa Dhahabu wa Georgia wa Karne ya 12

sourceText: His academic pursuits were initially stifled by the political turmoil of the Iconoclast controversy, and he faced jealousy and opposition.
translatedText: Shughuli zake za kielimu hapo awali zilizuiwa na msukosuko wa kisiasa wa mzozo wa Iconoclast, na alikabiliwa na wivu na upinzani.

sourceText: He gained the patronage of the powerful Bardas, who appointed him to re-found and lead the Magnaura School, making it a center of learning.
translatedText: Alipata udhamini wa Bardas mwenye nguvu, ambaye alimteua kupata tena na kuongoza Shule ya Magnaura, na kuifanya kituo cha kujifunza.

sourceText: 9th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 9

sourceText: Abai Qunanbaiuly
translatedText: Abai Qunanbaiuly

sourceText: Struggling against the oppressive policies of the Russian Empire and the rigid, outdated feudal traditions of his own society.
translatedText: Kupambana dhidi ya sera za ukandamizaji za Dola ya Urusi na mila ngumu, iliyopitwa na wakati ya jamii yake mwenyewe.

sourceText: He forged a new path by enlightening his people through poetry, philosophical works, and translations, creating a modern Kazakh cultural identity.
translatedText: Alibuni njia mpya kwa kuwaangazia watu wake kupitia ushairi, kazi za kifalsafa, na tafsiri, na kuunda utambulisho wa kitamaduni wa kisasa wa Kazakh.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Persecution, exile to Siberia, imprisonment, and the martyrdom of his family and followers for opposing church reforms.
translatedText: Mateso, uhamishoni Siberia, kufungwa gerezani, na kuuawa kwa ajili ya familia yake na wafuasi wake kwa ajili ya kupinga marekebisho ya kanisa.

sourceText: Found strength in unyielding faith, writing his influential autobiography, and becoming a martyr-saint for the Old Believers.
translatedText: Alipata nguvu katika imani isiyobadilika, kuandika tawasifu yake yenye ushawishi, na kuwa mtakatifu-mtakatifu kwa Waumini wa Kale.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: The internal fragmentation of his empire, constant resistance from external powers, and the relentless pressure of continuous warfare to maintain control.
translatedText: Mgawanyiko wa ndani wa himaya yake, upinzani wa mara kwa mara kutoka kwa nguvu za nje, na shinikizo lisilokoma la vita vinavyoendelea kudumisha udhibiti.

sourceText: Maintained imperial control by crushing rebellions and subjugating enemies with overwhelming military force and ruthless tactics.
translatedText: Ilidumisha udhibiti wa kifalme kwa kukandamiza waasi na kuwatiisha maadui kwa nguvu nyingi za kijeshi na mbinu za ukatili.

sourceText: 5th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 5

sourceText: Ahmad Yasawi
translatedText: Ahmad Yasawi

sourceText: The internal struggle between worldly knowledge and spiritual enlightenment, and the challenge of making the essence of Islam accessible to the common people.
translatedText: Mapambano ya ndani kati ya elimu ya dunia na mwanga wa kiroho, na changamoto ya kufanya dhati ya Uislamu ipatikane kwa watu wa kawaida.

sourceText: He channeled his spiritual insights into poetry, composing in the common Turkic language to make the profound concepts of Sufism understandable and relatable to all.
translatedText: Alielekeza umaizi wake wa kiroho katika ushairi, akitunga katika lugha ya kawaida ya Kituruki ili kufanya dhana za kina za Usufi zieleweke na kuhusianishwa na wote.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 12

sourceText: Anna Komnene
translatedText: Anna Komnene

sourceText: Sidelined from the imperial succession and implicated in a plot against her brother, she was stripped of her property and exiled to a monastery.
translatedText: Akiwa ametengwa na urithi wa kifalme na kuhusishwa katika njama dhidi ya kaka yake, alinyang'anywa mali yake na kupelekwa uhamishoni kwenye makao ya watawa.

sourceText: In exile, she channeled her grief into scholarship, composing her magnum opus, 'The Alexiad,' to immortalize her father's reign.
translatedText: Akiwa uhamishoni, alielekeza huzuni yake katika ufadhili wa masomo, akitunga opus yake kubwa, 'The Alexiad,' ili kuufanya utawala wa baba yake ufe.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 11-12

sourceText: Andrei Rublev
translatedText: Andrei Rublev

sourceText: The constant violence and instability of the Tatar Yoke, and the inner struggle between artistic vision and monastic humility.
translatedText: Vurugu za mara kwa mara na kutokuwa na utulivu wa Nira ya Kitatari, na mapambano ya ndani kati ya maono ya kisanii na unyenyekevu wa kimonaki.

sourceText: Finding solace and purpose in prayer, meditation, and the creation of art that sought to reveal divine harmony and peace to a troubled world.
translatedText: Kupata kitulizo na kusudi katika sala, kutafakari, na uundaji wa sanaa ambayo ilitaka kufunua upatano wa kimungu na amani kwa ulimwengu wenye shida.

sourceText: 14th-15th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14-15

sourceText: Alexander Nevsky
translatedText: Alexander Nevsky

sourceText: Navigating the immense pressure of protecting his realm from two fronts: the Catholic Crusaders from the West and the powerful Mongol Empire (the Golden Horde) from the East.
translatedText: Kupitia shinikizo kubwa la kulinda milki yake kutoka pande mbili: Wapiganaji wa Krusedi wa Kikatoliki kutoka Magharibi na Milki yenye nguvu ya Mongol (The Golden Horde) kutoka Mashariki.

sourceText: Employing a dual policy: decisively defeating Western invaders militarily while pragmatically submitting to the Mongol khans to preserve the Russian lands, people, and Orthodox faith.
translatedText: Kuajiri sera mbili: kwa kuwashinda wavamizi wa Magharibi kijeshi huku wakijisalimisha kwa vitendo kwa khans wa Mongol ili kuhifadhi ardhi ya Urusi, watu na imani ya Othodoksi.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Exile and strict censorship by the Tsar for my liberal political views, and constant social pressures that ultimately led to my death.
translatedText: Kuhamishwa na udhibiti mkali wa Tsar kwa maoni yangu ya kisiasa ya huria, na shinikizo za kijamii za mara kwa mara ambazo hatimaye zilisababisha kifo changu.

sourceText: I channeled my isolation and frustrations into my most creative periods, using folklore and history to craft a new, purely Russian literary voice.
translatedText: Nilielekeza kutengwa na kufadhaika kwangu katika vipindi vyangu vya ubunifu zaidi, kwa kutumia ngano na historia kuunda sauti mpya ya fasihi ya Kirusi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Alexios I Komnenos
translatedText: Alexios I Komnenos

sourceText: Inheriting a collapsing empire besieged by Normans, Pechenegs, and Seljuk Turks.
translatedText: Kurithi himaya inayoanguka iliyozingirwa na Wanormani, Pechenegs, na Waturuki wa Seljuk.

sourceText: Through shrewd diplomacy, military reforms, and leveraging the First Crusade, I stabilized the empire and began its recovery.
translatedText: Kupitia diplomasia ya busara, mageuzi ya kijeshi, na kutumia Vita vya Kwanza vya Msalaba, niliimarisha milki hiyo na kuanza kuimarika.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11-12

sourceText: Alisher Navoi
translatedText: Alisher Navoi

sourceText: The struggle to prove the literary value of Turkic languages in an environment dominated by the prestige of Persian literature.
translatedText: Mapambano ya kuthibitisha thamani ya fasihi ya lugha za Kituruki katika mazingira yaliyotawaliwa na ufahari wa fasihi ya Kiajemi.

sourceText: By writing masterpieces and treatises like 'The Comparison of the Two Languages', he demonstrated the richness of Turkic and established Chagatai as a respected literary language.
translatedText: Kwa kuandika kazi bora na risala kama vile 'Ulinganisho wa Lugha Mbili', alionyesha utajiri wa Kituruki na kuanzisha Kichagatai kama lugha ya fasihi inayoheshimika.

sourceText: Timurid Empire, 15th Century
translatedText: Dola ya Timurid, Karne ya 15

sourceText: Muhammad al-Bukhari
translatedText: Muhammad al-Bukhari

sourceText: Accused of heresy and faced political persecution, leading to his exile from major cities like Nishapur and his hometown of Bukhara.
translatedText: Alishutumiwa kwa uzushi na alikabiliwa na mateso ya kisiasa, na kusababisha uhamisho wake kutoka miji mikubwa kama Nishapur na mji wake wa Bukhara.

sourceText: He remained steadfast in his scholarly principles and dedicated himself to teaching and completing his life's work, which ultimately became his enduring legacy.
translatedText: Alibaki thabiti katika kanuni zake za kitaaluma na alijitolea kufundisha na kukamilisha kazi yake ya maisha, ambayo hatimaye ikawa urithi wake wa kudumu.

sourceText: 9th Century / Abbasid Caliphate
translatedText: Karne ya 9 / Ukhalifa wa Abbas

sourceText: Al-Farghani
translatedText: Al-Farghani

sourceText: The challenge of simplifying the complex astronomical theories of the ancient Greeks for a wider audience.
translatedText: Changamoto ya kurahisisha nadharia changamano za unajimu za Wagiriki wa kale kwa hadhira kubwa zaidi.

sourceText: By writing the 'Elements of Astronomy,' a concise and systematic summary of Ptolemy's Almagest.
translatedText: Kwa kuandika 'Elements of Astronomy,' muhtasari mfupi na wa utaratibu wa Almagest ya Ptolemy.

sourceText: 9th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 9

sourceText: Bloody succession wars against my brothers and constant threats from nomadic tribes like the Pechenegs.
translatedText: Vita vya mfululizo vya umwagaji damu dhidi ya ndugu zangu na vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa makabila ya kuhamahama kama vile Wapechenegs.

sourceText: I secured my throne through military victory and shrewd diplomacy, then focused on internal consolidation through law, education, and strategic alliances.
translatedText: Nilipata kiti changu cha enzi kupitia ushindi wa kijeshi na diplomasia ya busara, kisha nikazingatia uimarishaji wa ndani kupitia sheria, elimu, na ushirikiano wa kimkakati.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11

sourceText: Yermak Timofeyevich
translatedText: Yermak Timofeyevich

sourceText: Facing a vast, unknown wilderness and a numerically superior enemy with only a small band of men.
translatedText: Inakabiliwa na jangwa kubwa, lisilojulikana na adui mkuu wa nambari na kikundi kidogo cha wanaume.

sourceText: My campaign, though it cost me my life, opened Siberia for Russia, creating an empire stretching to the Pacific.
translatedText: Kampeni yangu, ingawa iligharimu maisha yangu, ilifungua Siberia kwa Urusi, na kuunda milki iliyoenea hadi Pasifiki.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 16

sourceText: Conflict with the imperial court, leading to two exiles and a harsh life away from his flock.
translatedText: Mgogoro na mahakama ya kifalme, na kusababisha wahamishwa wawili na maisha magumu mbali na kundi lake.

sourceText: Maintained his faith and influence through extensive letter-writing and theological work, even during his exile.
translatedText: Alidumisha imani na ushawishi wake kupitia uandishi wa barua nyingi na kazi ya kitheolojia, hata wakati wa uhamisho wake.

sourceText: Late 4th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Mwisho wa Milki ya Kirumi ya Karne ya 4

sourceText: The constant struggle between his duties as a ruler and his passion for science, culminating in a rebellion led by his own son.
translatedText: Mapambano ya mara kwa mara kati ya majukumu yake kama mtawala na shauku yake kwa sayansi, na kufikia kilele cha uasi ulioongozwa na mtoto wake mwenyewe.

sourceText: Though his political career ended in tragedy, his scientific legacy, particularly the 'Zij-i Sultani' star catalogue, influenced astronomy for centuries.
translatedText: Ingawa kazi yake ya kisiasa iliishia kwa msiba, urithi wake wa kisayansi, hasa orodha ya nyota ya 'Zij-i Sultani', iliathiri elimu ya nyota kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 15th Century Timurid Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Timurid ya Karne ya 15

sourceText: The devastating Nika riots nearly cost me my throne and burned much of Constantinople. Later, a terrible plague swept through the empire, decimating the population and weakening our strength.
translatedText: Ghasia mbaya za Nika karibu zilinigharimu kiti changu cha enzi na kuteketeza sehemu kubwa ya Constantinople. Baadaye, tauni ya kutisha ilipita katika himaya hiyo, ikiangamiza idadi ya watu na kudhoofisha nguvu zetu.

sourceText: With the counsel of my wife Theodora, I crushed the revolt. I then focused on rebuilding, codifying the law to create order, and commissioning the magnificent Hagia Sophia as a testament to God's glory and my power.
translatedText: Kwa ushauri wa mke wangu Theodora, niliponda uasi. Kisha nikakazia fikira kujenga upya, kutunga sheria ili kuleta utulivu, na kumwagiza Hagia Sophia mwenye fahari kama ushuhuda wa utukufu wa Mungu na nguvu zangu.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 6

sourceText: Ruling amidst constant threats from the Golden Horde, the Grand Duchy of Lithuania, and internal rivalries among Rus' princes.
translatedText: Kutawala huku kukiwa na vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa Golden Horde, Grand Duchy ya Lithuania, na mashindano ya ndani kati ya wakuu wa Rus.

sourceText: Through shrewd diplomacy, strategic military campaigns, and a relentless drive to centralize power, I unified the Russian lands and asserted our sovereignty.
translatedText: Kupitia diplomasia ya busara, kampeni za kimkakati za kijeshi, na msukumo usiokoma wa kuweka mamlaka, niliunganisha nchi za Urusi na kusisitiza enzi kuu yetu.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 15

sourceText: Isolation and betrayal by the boyars during his childhood, and the death of his beloved wife, Anastasia.
translatedText: Kutengwa na usaliti na wavulana wakati wa utoto wake, na kifo cha mke wake mpendwa, Anastasia.

sourceText: Seized absolute power, crushed the boyars, and centralized the state, expanding Russia into an empire.
translatedText: Kunyakua mamlaka kamili, kukandamiza vijana, na kuweka serikali kuu, na kupanua Urusi kuwa ufalme.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 16

sourceText: Ivan Mazepa
translatedText: Ivan Mazepa

sourceText: The crushing defeat at Poltava, which shattered my dream of an independent Ukraine and led to my being branded a traitor by the Russian Empire.
translatedText: Kushindwa vibaya sana huko Poltava, ambako kulikatiza ndoto yangu ya Ukrainia huru na kupelekea Milki ya Urusi kutajwa kuwa msaliti.

sourceText: Though I died in exile, my actions inspired generations of Ukrainians to fight for their sovereignty. My story became a powerful symbol of national resistance, immortalized in art and literature across Europe.
translatedText: Ingawa nilikufa uhamishoni, matendo yangu yalichochea vizazi vya Waukraine kupigania enzi kuu yao. Hadithi yangu ikawa ishara ya nguvu ya upinzani wa kitaifa, isiyoweza kufa katika sanaa na fasihi kote Uropa.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 17 - Mapema karne ya 18

sourceText: Ivan Franko
translatedText: Ivan Franko

sourceText: Political oppression by the Austro-Hungarian Empire, repeated imprisonment for socialist activities, and being ostracized from academia.
translatedText: Ukandamizaji wa kisiasa na Dola ya Austro-Hungarian, kufungwa mara kwa mara kwa shughuli za ujamaa, na kutengwa na wasomi.

sourceText: He channeled his struggles into a prolific output of literature, scholarship, and political writing, awakening the national consciousness and spirit of the Ukrainian people.
translatedText: Alielekeza mapambano yake katika pato kubwa la fasihi, usomi, na uandishi wa kisiasa, na kuamsha ufahamu wa kitaifa na roho ya watu wa Ukrain.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Ismail Gasprinsky
translatedText: Ismail Gasprinsky

sourceText: Facing oppression from the Russian Empire and resistance from conservative elements within Muslim society.
translatedText: Kukabiliana na ukandamizaji kutoka kwa Dola ya Urusi na upinzani kutoka kwa wahafidhina ndani ya jamii ya Kiislamu.

sourceText: Championed modernization and unity through his newspaper Terciman and the 'New Method' (Usul-i Jadid) of education.
translatedText: Alishinda usasa na umoja kupitia gazeti lake Terciman na 'Njia Mpya' (Usul-i Jadid) ya elimu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Jamshid al-Kashi
translatedText: Jamshid al-Kashi

sourceText: Early in my career, I struggled with poverty and a lack of patronage for my scientific pursuits.
translatedText: Mapema katika kazi yangu, nilipambana na umaskini na ukosefu wa wafadhili wa shughuli zangu za kisayansi.

sourceText: The invitation from Prince Ulugh Beg to join his new observatory in Samarkand provided me with the resources and stability to dedicate my life to science.
translatedText: Mwaliko kutoka kwa Prince Ulugh Beg kujiunga na chumba chake kipya cha uchunguzi huko Samarkand ulinipa rasilimali na uthabiti wa kujitolea maisha yangu kwa sayansi.

sourceText: 15th Century Timurid Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Timurid ya Karne ya 15

sourceText: Shoqan Walikhanov
translatedText: Shoqan Walikhanov

sourceText: The lonely struggle to preserve the identity and culture of my people amidst Russian imperial expansion, all while battling frail health which led to a tragically short life.
translatedText: Mapambano ya upweke ya kuhifadhi utambulisho na utamaduni wa watu wangu kati ya upanuzi wa kifalme wa Urusi, wakati wote nikipambana na afya dhaifu ambayo ilisababisha maisha mafupi ya kusikitisha.

sourceText: I leveraged my unique position as a Russian-educated Kazakh noble and officer to scientifically document and study the history, languages, and traditions of Central Asian peoples, giving them a voice in the world of scholarship.
translatedText: Nilichukua nafasi yangu ya kipekee kama afisa na afisa wa Kazakh aliyeelimishwa na Urusi kuandika na kusoma kisayansi historia, lugha, na mila za watu wa Asia ya Kati, nikiwapa sauti katika ulimwengu wa masomo.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Facing the political and religious turmoil of the Iconoclast controversy and forgoing a potential life as empress for one of spiritual devotion.
translatedText: Kukabiliana na msukosuko wa kisiasa na kidini wa pambano la Iconoclast na kuacha maisha yanayoweza kuwa ya kifalme kwa ajili ya kujitolea kiroho.

sourceText: She founded a convent, dedicating her life to God and channeling her intellect and artistry into creating a lasting legacy of hymns and poetry.
translatedText: Alianzisha nyumba ya watawa, akiweka maisha yake wakfu kwa Mungu na kuelekeza akili na usanii wake katika kuunda urithi wa kudumu wa nyimbo na mashairi.

sourceText: 9th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 9

sourceText: Constantine XI Palaiologos
translatedText: Constantine XI Palaiologos

sourceText: The profound isolation of leading a dying empire, betrayed by the divisions of Christendom and facing an overwhelming enemy.
translatedText: Kutengwa sana kwa kuongoza milki inayokufa, iliyosalitiwa na migawanyiko ya Jumuiya ya Wakristo na kukabili adui mkubwa sana.

sourceText: By choosing to die in battle alongside his soldiers and citizens, he transformed a military defeat into a legendary act of martyrdom and imperial dignity.
translatedText: Kwa kuchagua kufa vitani pamoja na askari na raia wake, aligeuza kushindwa kijeshi kuwa kitendo cha hadithi cha kifo cha kishahidi na heshima ya kifalme.

sourceText: Mid-15th Century
translatedText: Katikati ya karne ya 15

sourceText: Kublai Khan
translatedText: Kublai Khan

sourceText: Constant succession struggles with my own kin, the costly and difficult conquest of the Southern Song, and the devastating failures of my naval expeditions against Japan.
translatedText: Mapambano ya mfululizo ya mara kwa mara na jamaa zangu, ushindi wa gharama na mgumu wa Wimbo wa Kusini, na kushindwa vibaya kwa safari zangu za majini dhidi ya Japani.

sourceText: By adopting Chinese administrative systems, promoting trade and infrastructure like the Grand Canal, and embracing a policy of religious tolerance, I consolidated my rule and created a prosperous, multicultural empire.
translatedText: Kwa kupitisha mifumo ya utawala ya Kichina, kukuza biashara na miundo msingi kama vile Mfereji Mkuu, na kukumbatia sera ya uvumilivu wa kidini, niliunganisha utawala wangu na kuunda himaya yenye mafanikio, ya tamaduni nyingi.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Cultural shock and loneliness in a foreign land, and the political intrigues she faced as regent after her husband's death.
translatedText: Mshtuko wa kitamaduni na upweke katika nchi ya kigeni, na fitina za kisiasa alizokabiliana nazo kama kanuni baada ya kifo cha mumewe.

sourceText: Used her superior education and intellect to actively participate in French politics, securing her son Philip I's throne and proving her influence.
translatedText: Alitumia elimu yake ya juu na akili kushiriki kikamilifu katika siasa za Ufaransa, kupata kiti cha enzi cha mwanawe Philip I na kuthibitisha ushawishi wake.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11

sourceText: The brutal murder of her husband, Igor of Kyiv, by the Drevlian tribe.
translatedText: Mauaji ya kikatili ya mumewe, Igor wa Kyiv, na kabila la Drevlian.

sourceText: Securing her rule through a series of calculated and brutal acts of revenge, followed by significant administrative reforms and her eventual conversion to Christianity.
translatedText: Kulinda utawala wake kupitia mfululizo wa vitendo vilivyohesabiwa na vya kikatili vya kulipiza kisasi, na kufuatiwa na mageuzi makubwa ya kiutawala na hatimaye uongofu wake hadi Ukristo.

sourceText: 10th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 10

sourceText: Taras Shevchenko
translatedText: Taras Shevchenko

sourceText: Born into serfdom, years of exile, and a strict ban on writing and painting.
translatedText: Alizaliwa katika serfdom, miaka ya uhamishoni, na marufuku kali ya kuandika na uchoraji.

sourceText: Redeemed from serfdom by friends, he continued to write in secret during exile, becoming a symbol of Ukrainian resilience.
translatedText: Alikombolewa kutoka kwa serfdom na marafiki, aliendelea kuandika kwa siri wakati wa uhamisho, na kuwa ishara ya ujasiri wa Kiukreni.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Facing opposition from nobles who questioned a woman's right to rule, and enduring a tumultuous first marriage that ended in a coup attempt.
translatedText: Kukabiliana na upinzani kutoka kwa wakuu ambao walitilia shaka haki ya mwanamke kutawala, na kuvumilia ndoa ya kwanza yenye misukosuko ambayo ilimalizika kwa jaribio la mapinduzi.

sourceText: Asserting her authority with political acumen, leading her armies to victory, and forging a successful second marriage to secure her dynasty and kingdom.
translatedText: Akithibitisha mamlaka yake kwa ustadi wa kisiasa, akiongoza majeshi yake kwenye ushindi, na kuanzisha ndoa ya pili yenye mafanikio ili kupata nasaba na ufalme wake.

sourceText: 12th-13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 12-13

sourceText: Enduring scorn for my humble origins as an actress and facing constant threats from political rivals within the court.
translatedText: Kuvumilia dharau kwa asili yangu duni kama mwigizaji na kukabiliwa na vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa wapinzani wa kisiasa ndani ya mahakama.

sourceText: By earning the absolute trust and love of Emperor Justinian, I proved my intelligence and resolve, establishing myself as his co-ruler and most trusted advisor.
translatedText: Kwa kupata imani na upendo kamili wa Maliki Justinian, nilithibitisha akili na azimio langu, nikijifanya kuwa mtawala mwenza wake na mshauri anayetegemewa zaidi.

sourceText: 6th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 6

sourceText: Life as an itinerant artist, leaving his Byzantine homeland to work in foreign lands, and the artistic struggle to forge a unique, expressive style within the strict conventions of icon painting.
translatedText: Maisha kama msanii msafiri, akiacha nchi yake ya Byzantine kufanya kazi katika nchi za kigeni, na mapambano ya kisanii ya kuunda mtindo wa kipekee, wa kuelezea ndani ya mikataba kali ya uchoraji wa icons.

sourceText: Achieving great fame and patronage across the Russian principalities, creating masterpieces that defined an era, and securing his legacy by mentoring the next generation of masters, like Andrei Rublev.
translatedText: Kufikia umaarufu mkubwa na ulinzi katika wakuu wa Urusi, kuunda kazi bora ambazo zilifafanua enzi, na kupata urithi wake kwa kushauri kizazi kijacho cha mabwana, kama Andrei Rublev.

sourceText: Late 14th - Early 15th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 14 - Mapema karne ya 15

sourceText: Witnessing the subjugation and dispersal of the Turkic people under the rule of the Tang Dynasty.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kutiishwa na kutawanywa kwa watu wa Kituruki chini ya utawala wa nasaba ya Tang.

sourceText: By persuading Ilterish Khagan to revolt and using his military and diplomatic genius to establish and stabilize the Second Turkic Khaganate.
translatedText: Kwa kumshawishi Ilterish Khagan kuasi na kutumia fikra zake za kijeshi na kidiplomasia kuanzisha na kuleta utulivu Khaganate ya Pili ya Turkic.

sourceText: 7th-8th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 7-8

sourceText: Suffered severe injuries in his youth that left him with a permanent limp, leading to the Persian nickname 'Timur-i-Lang' (Timur the Lame). He also lacked a direct bloodline to Genghis Khan, a crucial source of legitimacy.
translatedText: Alipata majeraha mabaya katika ujana wake ambayo yalimwacha na kilema cha kudumu, na kusababisha jina la utani la Kiajemi 'Timur-i-Lang' (Timur the Lame). Pia hakuwa na mstari wa damu moja kwa moja kwa Genghis Khan, chanzo muhimu cha uhalali.

sourceText: He compensated for his physical disability with immense willpower and strategic brilliance. To bolster his legitimacy, he married a princess of Chagatai-Khanate, a descendant of Genghis Khan, adopting the title 'Guregen' (royal son-in-law), and styled himself as a champion of Islam.
translatedText: Alifidia ulemavu wake wa kimwili kwa utashi mkubwa na ustadi wa kimkakati. Ili kuimarisha uhalali wake, alioa binti wa kifalme wa Chagatai-Khanate, mzao wa Genghis Khan, akichukua jina la 'Guregen' (mkwe wa kifalme), na akajifanya kuwa bingwa wa Uislamu.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14

sourceText: Petro Mohyla
translatedText: Petro Mohyla

sourceText: The immense pressure to restore the identity of a divided Orthodox Church, caught between the influences of Catholicism and Protestantism.
translatedText: Shinikizo kubwa la kurejesha utambulisho wa Kanisa Othodoksi lililogawanyika, lililoshikwa kati ya uvutano wa Ukatoliki na Uprotestanti.
